page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor using the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational capabilities. The server achieves a PassMark CPU score of 105,569.39, placing it in the top 10% tier, with similarly high performance in extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks fall into the top 25% tier, with values of 2,282.88 and 94,844.99 respectively. Memory bandwidth is also robust, measuring 7,051.06 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 345.73 GB/sec for large uncached read blocks. In data processing, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance with 35.35 GB/sec in single-threaded gzip compression and 556.8 GB/sec in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

While the server lacks local NVMe storage and integrated GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable, allowing external storage attachments. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring direct-attached physical disks or hardware-accelerated graphics. However, the high resource density of 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM provides qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale, CPU-bound enterprise applications. This configuration is suited for high-throughput static web serving (benchmarked at 124.31 GB/sec), Redis database operations (achieving 152.07 million operations per second), and CPU-based LLM inference, where it processes prompts at 242.27 tokens/sec for medium 7B models.
description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores), 384.0 GB of RAM, and a 480 MB L3 cache. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks place this server in the top 10% tier for PassMark CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations, which reach 152.07 million operations per second. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and prompt processing speeds for large language models. This resource profile supports high-throughput static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and no local storage. It delivers top-tier CPU and database performance in benchmarks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose VM with 384 GB RAM, featuring top-tier CPU benchmarks and Premium Storage capability.
tagline: A high-density, 96-vCPU general-purpose instance delivering top-tier CPU performance and massive memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6973P-C
384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier CPU performance with a PassMark score of 105,569.39
Premium Storage capable but lacks local NVMe storage and GPUs
Suited for high-throughput web serving, Redis databases, and CPU-based LLM inference
